messageId.description: Choose from the list, or specify an ID using an expression

threadNotice.displayName: To reply to an existing thread, specify the exact subject title of that thread.

attachmentsUi.description: Array of supported attachments to add to the message

property.description: Add the field name from the input node. Multiple properties can be set separated by comma.
Add the field name from the input node. Multiple properties can be set separated by comma.

bccList.description: The email addresses of the blind copy recipients. Multiple addresses can be separated by a comma. e.g. jay@getsby.com, jon@smith.com.

ccList.description: The email addresses of the copy recipients. Multiple addresses can be separated by a comma. e.g. jay@getsby.com, jon@smith.com.

fromAlias.description: Select the alias to send the email from. Choose from the list, or specify an ID using an expression.

replyTo.description: The email address that the reply message is sent to

threadId.description: The identifier of the thread to attach the draft

sendTo.description: The email addresses of the recipients. Multiple addresses can be separated by a comma. e.g. jay@getsby.com, jon@smith.com.

dataPropertyAttachmentsPrefixName.description: Prefix for name of the binary property to which to write the attachment. An index starting with 0 will be added. So if name is 'attachment_' the first attachment is saved to 'attachment_0'.

downloadAttachments.description: Whether the email's attachments will be downloaded and included in the output

includeSpamTrash.description: Whether to include messages from SPAM and TRASH in the results

labelListVisibility.description: The visibility of the label in the label list in the Gmail web interface

messageListVisibility.description: The visibility of messages with this label in the message list in the Gmail web interface

appendAttribution.description: Whether to include the phrase "This message was sent automatically with n8n" to the end of the message

senderName.description: The name displayed in your contacts inboxes

replyToSenderOnly.description: Whether to reply to the sender only or to the entire list of recipients

limitWaitTime.description: Whether to limit the time this node should wait for a user response before execution resumes

limitType.description: Sets the condition for the execution to resume. Can be a specified date or after some time.

afterTimeInterval.description: Waits for a certain amount of time

atSpecifiedTime.description: Waits until the set date and time to continue

maxDateAndTime.description: Continue execution after the specified date and time

limitType.description: Sets the condition for the execution to resume. Can be a specified date or after some time.

afterTimeInterval.description: Waits for a certain amount of time

atSpecifiedTime.description: Waits until the set date and time to continue

maxDateAndTime.description: Continue execution after the specified date and time

responseFormTitle.description: Title of the form that the user can access to provide their response

responseFormDescription.description: Description of the form that the user can access to provide their response

responseFormCustomCss.description: Override default styling of the response form with CSS

replyToRecipientsOnly.description: Whether to exclude the sender from the reply

returnOnlyMessages.description: Whether to return only thread messages

returnAll.description: Whether to return all results or only up to a given limit

limit.description: Max number of results to return

sendTo.description: The email addresses of the recipients. Multiple addresses can be separated by a comma. e.g. jay@getsby.com, jon@smith.com.

simple.description: Whether to return a simplified version of the response instead of the raw data

filtersNotice.displayName: Fetching a lot of messages may take a long time. Consider using filters to speed things up

includeSpamTrash.description: Whether to include threads from SPAM and TRASH in the results

labelIds.description: Only return threads with labels that match all of the specified label IDs. Choose from the list, or specify IDs using an expression.

q.description: Only return messages matching the specified query

receivedAfter.description: Get all emails received after the specified date. In an expression you can set date using string in ISO format or a timestamp in miliseconds.

receivedBefore.description: Get all emails received before the specified date. In an expression you can set date using string in ISO format or a timestamp in miliseconds.

sender.description: Sender name or email to filter by

labelIds.description: Choose from the list, or specify IDs using an expression

approval.description: User can approve/disapprove from within the message

freeText.description: User can submit a response via a form

customForm.description: User can submit a response via a custom form

fieldName.description: The name of the field, used in input attributes and referenced by the workflow

fieldLabel.description: Label that appears above the input field

fieldType.description: The type of field to add to the form

elementName.description: Optional field. It can be used to include the html in the output.

placeholder.description: Sample text to display inside the field

defaultValue.description: Default value(s) that will be pre-selected. Must match one or multiple of the option labels. Separate multiple pre-selected options with a comma.

fieldValue.description: Input value can be set here or will be passed as a query parameter via Field Name if no value is set

multiselectLegacyNotice.displayName: Multiple Choice is a legacy option, please use Checkboxes or Radio Buttons field type instead

multiselect.description: Whether to allow the user to select multiple options from the dropdown list

html.description: HTML elements to display on the form page

multipleFiles.description: Whether to allow the user to select multiple files from the file input or just one

acceptFileTypes.description: Comma-separated list of allowed file extensions

formatDate.displayName: The displayed date is formatted based on the locale of the user's browser

requiredField.description: Whether to require the user to enter a value for this field before submitting the form

threadId.description: The ID of the thread you are operating on
